pulse: The Industrials sector currently trades at a median EV/EBITDA of 16.7x and a P/E of 29.1x, both above the S&P 500 forward P/E of 21.6x and reflecting its higher growth profile with a median revenue growth of 7.8%. Notably, GE trades at a premium of 34.5x EV/EBITDA with a strong revenue growth of 17.6% and a margin leader's profit margin of 19.0%, contrasting with F’s significant underperformance at 35.9x EV/EBITDA and a negative profit margin of -4.4%. As the earnings cycle progresses, investors should monitor potential earnings revisions in cyclicals like GE and CAT that are benefiting from increased capital expenditures in infrastructure, which could further elevate their valuations amidst improved macroeconomic conditions.
